walkthrough.title: Assemble the Race Track
walkthrough.text: Let's get Camel Up set up. First, find the track pieces and connect them together to form the racing loop. The track has sixteen numbered spaces forming a big circle. Lay it out in the center of the table where everyone can see it.

walkthrough.title: Build the Pyramid
walkthrough.text: Now grab the cardboard pyramid dice shaker and assemble it. This is the coolest component in the game. It has a special mechanism that dispenses exactly one die at a time when you shake it and press the lever.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Leg Betting Tiles
walkthrough.text: For each of the five camel colors, find the matching stack of leg betting tiles. Each color has five tiles with values five, three, two, two, and two. Stack them in descending order with the five on top, and place each stack near the track where everyone can reach them.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Overall Race Bet Areas
walkthrough.text: Create two face-down pile areas near the board. One is for overall winner bets and the other is for overall loser bets. Label them or just agree on which is which. Cards placed here stay face-down until the end of the race.

walkthrough.title: Hand Out Player Components
walkthrough.text: Each player receives three Egyptian pound coins to start. Each player also takes one double-sided desert tile and a set of five overall race betting cards showing all five camel colors. Keep your betting cards hidden from other players.

walkthrough.title: Place Coins in the Bank
walkthrough.text: Put all the remaining Egyptian pound coins in a central supply that everyone can reach. This is the bank. You will be earning and spending coins from this supply throughout the game. Coins come in denominations of one and five.

walkthrough.title: Place the Camels
walkthrough.text: Now let's get the camels on the track. Load all five colored dice into the pyramid, then roll each die once. Place each camel on the space matching its die roll, which will be space one, two, or three. If multiple camels land on the same space, stack them with later-rolled camels on top.

walkthrough.title: Reload and Start
walkthrough.text: After placing all five camels, put all five dice back into the pyramid. Give the starting player marker to the youngest player or choose randomly. The first player takes the first turn of the first leg, and we are ready to race. Play goes clockwise.

Play clockwise, first leg begins

walkthrough.title: Game Structure: Legs and the Race
walkthrough.text: Camel Up is a race played over multiple legs. A leg ends when all five dice have been shaken out of the pyramid. The entire race ends when any camel crosses or lands on space sixteen. Think of each leg as a mini-round of betting opportunities before the next burst of camel movement.

walkthrough.title: Your Turn: Pick One Action
walkthrough.text: On your turn, you choose exactly one of four actions. You can take a leg betting tile, place your desert tile on the track, roll a die from the pyramid, or place an overall race bet. After you complete your action, play passes clockwise to the next player.

walkthrough.title: Action: Take a Leg Betting Tile
walkthrough.text: Pick up the top tile from any camel color's stack. You are betting that this camel will be in first place at the end of the current leg. The first tile available is worth five coins if you are right, then three, then two. If that camel finishes second instead, you earn just one coin. If it finishes in any other position, you lose one coin.

walkthrough.title: Action: Place Your Desert Tile
walkthrough.text: Put your desert tile on any empty track space that has no camel and no other desert tile on it. You cannot place it on space one. Choose which side faces up. The oasis side has a plus one, and the mirage side has a minus one. Whenever any camel lands on your tile, you collect one coin from the bank no matter which side is showing.

walkthrough.title: Desert Tile Effects
walkthrough.text: When a camel lands on the oasis side of a desert tile, it moves one additional space forward and goes on top of any camels already on that new space. When a camel lands on the mirage side, it moves one space backward instead and goes to the bottom of any stack on that space. Desert tiles can dramatically change the race.

walkthrough.title: Action: Roll a Pyramid Die
walkthrough.text: Shake the pyramid and pop out one random die. Move the matching camel forward the number of spaces shown on that die, which will be one, two, or three. You collect one coin from the bank just for rolling. The rolled die stays outside the pyramid for the rest of the leg.

walkthrough.title: Camel Stacking and Carrying
walkthrough.text: This is the heart of Camel Up. When a camel moves onto a space that already has camels, it lands on top of the existing stack. When a camel moves, it carries every camel that is stacked on top of it, but leaves behind any camels below it. The whole group moves together and lands on top at the destination. The camel highest in a stack on the farthest space is in first place.

walkthrough.title: Action: Place an Overall Race Bet
walkthrough.text: Take one of your five betting cards and place it face-down on either the overall winner pile or the overall loser pile. You are betting on which camel will be in first or last place when the entire race ends. The earlier you place a correct bet, the more it pays. First correct bet earns eight coins, then five, then three, then two, then one.

walkthrough.title: End of a Leg
walkthrough.text: When the fifth and final die is shaken out of the pyramid, the current leg ends immediately after that camel moves. First, determine which camel is in first place and score all leg betting tiles. Then return all desert tiles to their owners. Finally, put all five dice back in the pyramid and pass the starting player marker clockwise.

walkthrough.title: Scoring Leg Betting Tiles
walkthrough.text: Look at which camel is farthest along the track. If two camels share a space, the one on top of the stack is ahead. For each leg betting tile, check if it matches the first-place camel. If it matches first place, the holder earns the coin value printed on the tile. If it matches second place, the holder earns one coin. If it matches any other position, the holder pays one coin back to the bank.

walkthrough.title: End of the Race
walkthrough.text: The race ends the moment any camel crosses or lands on space sixteen. Finish scoring that final leg's betting tiles as normal. Then flip over the overall winner pile and reveal bets one by one from first placed to last placed. The first correct bet for the winning camel earns eight coins, the second correct earns five, then three, two, and one. Every incorrect bet in the pile loses one coin.

walkthrough.title: Scoring Overall Loser Bets
walkthrough.text: Now flip over the overall loser pile and do the same thing. The camel in last place is the target. The first correct bet earns eight coins, then five, three, two, and one. Incorrect bets lose one coin each. The loser bet is just as valuable as the winner bet, so do not ignore the back of the pack.

walkthrough.title: Counting Coins and Winning
walkthrough.text: Every player counts up their total Egyptian pounds. The player with the most coins wins the game. If there is a tie, the tied player with the most remaining overall bet cards wins. If it is still tied, those players share the victory.

summary.title: Game Structure: Legs and the Race
bullets: Race = multiple legs; leg ends when all 5 dice are rolled
Race ends when any camel crosses/reaches space 16

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Races
walkthrough.text: Welcome to your first race of Camel Up! In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first leg with four example players. Each player will try different actions so you can see how everything connects. Follow along even if you have a different number of players at your table.

walkthrough.title: The Starting Positions
walkthrough.text: After rolling dice to place the camels, let's say Orange and Blue are on space two with Orange on top, Green is alone on space three, and White and Yellow are on space one with Yellow on top. Notice that Green is technically in first place since it is farthest forward, but Orange has great carry potential sitting on top of Blue.

walkthrough.title: Player 1: Takes a Leg Betting Tile
walkthrough.text: Player 1 looks at the board and sees Green out in front on space three. Without hesitation, they grab the top leg betting tile for Green, which is the five-coin tile. If Green finishes the leg in first place, Player 1 earns five coins. That is the most valuable tile in the stack, and it is gone now.

walkthrough.title: Why Betting Early Pays Off
walkthrough.text: The five-coin tile is only available to the first person who bets on that camel. After that, the best remaining tile is worth three, then two. If you wait too long to bet, the reward shrinks while the risk stays the same. When you see a strong opportunity, grab it before someone else does.

walkthrough.title: Player 2: Rolls a Pyramid Die
walkthrough.text: Player 2 is not confident about any particular camel yet, so they shake the pyramid and out pops the Blue die showing a three. Blue moves forward three spaces, carrying Orange on top of it since Orange was stacked above Blue. Both camels jump from space two all the way to space five, with Orange still on top. Player 2 collects one coin from the bank for rolling.

walkthrough.title: The Power of Stacking
walkthrough.text: Notice what just happened. Orange did not roll at all, but it got a free ride to space five and is now in first place because it was sitting on top of Blue. This is the most important concept in Camel Up. A camel on top of a stack gets carried forward for free whenever the camel below it moves. Always think about who is carrying whom.

walkthrough.title: Player 3: Places a Desert Tile
walkthrough.text: Player 3 decides to place their desert tile on the oasis side on space six, right in front of the Orange-Blue stack. If any camel lands on space six, it will bounce one extra space forward to space seven, and Player 3 will collect one coin from the bank. Smart placement right in the path of the leaders.

walkthrough.title: Mistake: Taking a Low-Value Tile
walkthrough.text: Player 4 grabs the three-coin leg betting tile for Orange, hoping to play it safe. But the five-coin Orange tile is still available on top of the stack. There is no reason to leave the better-paying tile for someone else when you are making the exact same bet. If you are confident enough to bet on a camel, always take the top tile for maximum value.

walkthrough.title: Player 1 Rolls and Player 2 Bets Big
walkthrough.text: Player 1 shakes the pyramid and out comes the Orange die showing a two. Orange moves forward from space five to space seven, pulling further ahead. Player 2 has been watching Orange surge and decides to go big. They place their Orange betting card face-down on the overall winner pile. If Orange wins the entire race, Player 2 earns eight coins as the first correct bet.

walkthrough.title: Mistake: Too Many Overall Bets
walkthrough.text: Player 3 gets excited and places an overall winner bet on Green, then on their next turn places another on Blue. That is two of their five betting cards committed in the first leg with barely any information. Each wrong overall bet costs one coin, and those penalties add up fast. It is usually better to wait until the second or third leg and place one or two confident bets rather than scattering cards everywhere.

walkthrough.title: Mistake: Ignoring the Stacks
walkthrough.text: Three dice remain in the pyramid: Green, White, and Yellow. Player 4 bets on White to win the leg, looking only at the spaces. But White is on space one underneath Yellow. Even if White rolls a three, it only reaches space four. Meanwhile, Green on space three could roll a three and reach space six. In Camel Up, a camel buried at the bottom of a stack can only move by its own die roll. Always check both the space number and the stack position before you bet.

walkthrough.title: Finishing the Leg
walkthrough.text: The remaining dice get rolled over the next few turns. Green rolls a two and moves to space five. Yellow rolls a one and moves to space two. White rolls a three and moves to space four. All five dice are out of the pyramid, so the leg is over.

walkthrough.title: Leg Scoring Results
walkthrough.text: Orange is on space seven, the farthest forward, so Orange wins the leg. Blue is on space five under Green, so Green is second because it is on top of the stack. Player 1 bet on Green for five coins but Green came in second, so Player 1 earns just one coin. Player 4 bet on Orange for three coins and earns those three, but also bet on White which finished fourth, losing one coin.

walkthrough.title: Mistake: Wasting the Desert Tile
walkthrough.text: Looking back at the leg, Player 3 placed their desert tile on space six but no camel actually landed there. Zero income from a misplaced tile. A better placement would have been space four or five, right where the cluster of camels was heading. When placing your desert tile, count the remaining dice and think about where camels are most likely to land. A tile that gets landed on even once per leg is steady income over the whole race.

walkthrough.title: Reset for the Next Leg
walkthrough.text: Now we clean up for the next leg. All desert tiles return to their owners and all five dice go back into the pyramid. The leg betting tile stacks reset to full, so the five-coin tile is available again for each camel. The starting player marker passes clockwise. A fresh leg means fresh betting opportunities.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: Congratulations, you have just played through your first leg of Camel Up! You saw all four actions in play, learned how stacking changes everything, and watched common mistakes unfold. Remember: bet early for maximum value, read the stacks before you bet, be selective with overall bets, and place desert tiles where camels will actually land.

walkthrough.title: Before You Race
walkthrough.text:
Before you start a full game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics and common pitfalls to avoid.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to game terms and scoring rules.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

summary.title: Before You Race
bullets: Check out General Tips → strategy basics for beginners
Reference the Appendix → quick-reference for terms and scoring
Ask your personal GM Guide → tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before we talk strategy, here are the biggest mistakes new players make. First, taking low-value leg betting tiles when the five-coin tile is still available for that camel. Second, ignoring stacking dynamics and betting on camels buried at the bottom of a stack. Third, scattering too many overall race bets and racking up penalties from wrong guesses. Fourth, placing desert tiles where no camel is likely to land. Keep these in mind and you will stay out of the common traps.

walkthrough.title: Bet Early or Roll
walkthrough.text: In Camel Up, information is everything, and the first player to act on information gets the biggest reward. If you see a camel with a clear advantage at the start of a leg, grab the five-coin tile immediately. If no camel looks obviously strong, roll the pyramid die instead. Rolling gives you one guaranteed coin and reveals new information that helps you make better bets on future turns. The worst thing you can do is sit on the fence and bet late for a measly two-coin tile.

walkthrough.title: Read the Stacks
walkthrough.text: Stacking is what makes Camel Up unpredictable and exciting. A camel sitting on top of a large stack is in an incredible position because every die roll for camels below it gives it a free ride forward. Conversely, a camel at the bottom of a stack can only advance by its own die roll and it has to carry everyone above it. Before you bet, always check the vertical order of every stack on the board, not just the horizontal positions.

walkthrough.title: Count the Dice
walkthrough.text: The pyramid starts each leg with five dice, and each die shows one, two, or three. As dice are rolled, you gain valuable information. If the Blue die has already come out, Blue cannot move again this leg. If only two dice remain, you can narrow down which camels will still move and by how much. Smart players count the remaining dice and calculate which outcomes would help or hurt their bets before committing coins.

walkthrough.title: Desert Tile Placement Strategy
walkthrough.text: Your desert tile is a passive income tool, but only if camels actually land on it. Place it one to three spaces ahead of the largest group of camels, since dice rolls range from one to three. The oasis side is great for pushing your favored camel farther ahead. The mirage side is a sneaky way to pull a leading camel backward, especially if you bet on a different camel to win. Reposition your tile each leg as the pack moves.

walkthrough.title: Overall Bet Timing
walkthrough.text: Overall race bets for winner and loser can pay up to eight coins, which is the biggest single payout in the game. But each wrong bet costs you one coin, and those penalties add up. The sweet spot is usually after the first or second leg, when you can see which camels are consistently leading or trailing. Place one or two high-confidence bets rather than gambling on three or four long shots.

walkthrough.title: Don't Forget the Loser Bet
walkthrough.text: New players often focus entirely on which camel will win and forget that the overall loser bet pays exactly the same rewards. If one camel is clearly stuck at the back of the pack with no favorable stacking, that is an eight-coin opportunity waiting for you. Track the last-place camel just as carefully as the leader.

walkthrough.title: Adapt Every Leg
walkthrough.text: Camel Up changes dramatically from leg to leg as camels shuffle positions and stacks reform. A camel that dominated the first leg might get buried at the bottom of a stack in the second. Do not get attached to one camel just because it did well earlier. Reassess the board at the start of every leg, check the stacking order, and make fresh decisions based on the current state of the race.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Don't take low-value tiles when the 5-coin tile is available
Don't ignore stacking — bottom camels are stuck, top camels ride free
Don't scatter too many overall bets — wrong guesses cost 1 coin each
Don't place desert tiles where no camel will land

summary.title: Adapt Every Leg

Reassess board at the start of every leg
Make fresh decisions based on current state, not past performance

walkthrough.title: Probability-Based Leg Betting
walkthrough.text: Each die has three faces showing one, two faces showing two, and one face showing three. That means the average roll is about 1.83 spaces. When deciding whether to take a leg betting tile, count the remaining dice and figure out the maximum and minimum each camel can advance. If a camel is three or more spaces ahead and only one die remains that could catch it, that is a very safe bet. The more dice left, the more variance there is and the riskier your bet becomes.

walkthrough.title: Stack Manipulation
walkthrough.text: Advanced players do not just read the stacks, they actively try to manipulate them. If your favored camel is behind another camel on the same space, rolling that front camel's die would separate them and leave yours behind. But if your camel is on top, rolling the bottom camel's die carries yours forward for free. Use your rolling turns strategically to set up favorable stacking rather than just rolling for the one-coin income.

walkthrough.title: Mirage Tile Trap
walkthrough.text: The mirage side of a desert tile is one of the most powerful tools in the game when used correctly. Place it one space ahead of a leading camel so that when it rolls a one, it lands on the mirage and gets pushed back. Even better, the camel goes to the bottom of the stack on the space it gets pushed to. This can completely reverse a camel's position from first to buried. Use the mirage to protect your bets by sabotaging rival camels.

walkthrough.title: Multi-Leg Income Optimization
walkthrough.text: Coins earned early in the game are not worth more than coins earned late, but they let you take bigger risks with overall bets. The players who consistently earn two to three coins per leg from rolling and desert tile income build a comfortable cushion. That cushion means they can afford a wrong overall bet or two without falling behind. Prioritize steady income in the early legs and swing for big overall bets in the later legs when you have more information.

walkthrough.title: Overall Bet Timing Windows
walkthrough.text: The optimal time to place an overall winner or loser bet is after the second leg. By then, one or two camels have usually established a lead or fallen behind, and you have seen enough stacking patterns to make an informed guess. Placing a bet after the first leg is riskier but earns you the eight-coin first-correct slot before cautious players commit. Waiting until the third leg is safer but the best payout slots may be taken. Weigh your confidence against the remaining payout slots.

walkthrough.title: Reading Other Players' Bets
walkthrough.text: Pay attention to what your opponents are doing. If three players have all taken leg betting tiles for the same camel, that tells you two things. First, those tiles are depleted and less valuable now. Second, those players might use their desert tiles and rolling decisions to help that camel. You can use this to your advantage by betting on a different camel and using your desert tile to sabotage the popular pick. In a game of limited information, watching your opponents is almost as valuable as watching the camels.

walkthrough.title: Endgame Positioning
walkthrough.text: As camels approach space sixteen, the game enters its most volatile phase. A single lucky roll can send a stack of three camels across the finish line, completely reshuffling the order. In these final legs, leg betting tiles become extremely safe because the range of possible outcomes is narrow. Meanwhile, if you have not placed your overall bets yet, this is your last chance, and the remaining payout slots will be smaller. The best players have their overall bets locked in by this point and are maximizing safe leg bets for guaranteed income.

walkthrough.title: Player Count Adjustments
walkthrough.text: Camel Up plays differently at different player counts and strong players adjust. With three or four players, leg betting tiles last longer, so you have more time to gather information before betting. With six to eight players, tiles get snatched up fast and rolling to collect one coin becomes less attractive because someone else will grab the five-coin tile on their turn. At higher player counts, bet more aggressively early and focus on overall race bets where the payout ceiling is higher.

summary.title: Probability-Based Leg Betting
bullets: Die faces: 1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 3 → average ~1.83 spaces
More remaining dice = more variance = riskier bets
Camel 3+ spaces ahead with 1 die left = very safe bet

summary.title: Stack Manipulation
bullets: Roll bottom camel's die to carry your camel on top forward
Avoid rolling if it would separate your camel from a beneficial stack
Use rolling turns to set up favorable stacking, not just for 1-coin income

summary.title: Mirage Tile Trap
bullets: Place mirage 1 space ahead of a leading rival camel
Camel rolls 1 → lands on mirage → pushed back to bottom of stack
Can completely reverse a camel's position from 1st to buried

summary.title: Multi-Leg Income Optimization

Cushion absorbs wrong overall bets without falling behind
Steady income early → swing for big overall bets later

bullets: After leg 2 = optimal balance of info and payout
After leg 1 = riskier but claims the 8-coin slot early
After leg 3 = safer but best slots may be taken

summary.title: Reading Other Players' Bets

summary.title: Player Count Adjustments
bullets: 3-4 players: tiles last longer, more time to gather info
6-8 players: tiles vanish fast, bet more aggressively early
Higher counts → focus on overall bets (higher payout ceiling)

entries.definition: A segment of the race lasting from when the pyramid is loaded until all five dice have been rolled. Leg betting tiles are scored at the end of each leg, desert tiles are returned, and dice are reloaded.
The entire game from start to finish. The race consists of multiple legs and ends when any camel crosses or lands on space sixteen. Overall winner and loser bets are scored at the end of the race.

When a camel lands on an occupied space, it goes on top of the existing camels. The camel highest in a stack on the farthest space is in first place. Stacking is the core mechanic that creates uncertainty and excitement.
When a camel moves, it carries all camels stacked on top of it. Camels below the moving camel stay in place. The entire sub-stack lands on top of any camels at the destination.
The cardboard pyramid that holds the five dice. Shake and press the lever to release one random die at a time. Reloaded with all five dice at the start of each leg.
The currency of the game, in denominations of one and five. Players start with three coins. Earned through bets, rolling, and desert tiles. Most coins at game end wins.

A double-sided tile each player owns. Oasis side grants plus one forward movement; mirage side causes minus one backward movement. Placed on any empty space except space one. Earns the owner one coin whenever a camel lands on it.

Pick the top tile from any camel color's stack. Betting that camel will finish first in the current leg. First tile is worth five, then three, then two. Pays tile value for first place, one coin for second, costs one coin for any other position.

Put your desert tile on any empty track space with no camel, no other desert tile, and not space one. Choose oasis or mirage side. Earn one coin from the bank whenever any camel lands on it. Returned to you at end of each leg.

Shake the pyramid to release one random die. Move the matching colored camel forward the number shown (one, two, or three). Collect one coin from the bank. The die stays outside the pyramid for the rest of the leg.

Put one of your five betting cards face-down on the overall winner or loser pile. Earlier correct bets pay more: eight, five, three, two, one coins. Incorrect bets lose one coin each. Cards are revealed and scored at race end.

Tiles stacked in descending value (five, three, two, two, two) for each camel color. Taking a tile is a bet that the camel will win the current leg. Scored at the end of each leg and returned to form new stacks.

A face-down card placed on the winner pile predicting which camel will be in first place at race end. First correct bet earns eight coins. Incorrect bets cost one coin. Each player has one card per camel color.

A face-down card placed on the loser pile predicting which camel will be in last place at race end. Pays identically to the winner bet: eight, five, three, two, one coins for correct bets in order. Incorrect bets cost one coin.

At the end of each leg, determine the first-place camel (farthest space, highest in stack if tied). Matching leg tiles pay face value for first, one coin for second, and cost one coin for any other position.

At race end, winner and loser piles are revealed in order. Correct bets pay eight, five, three, two, one coins based on placement order. Incorrect bets cost one coin each. Both piles scored the same way.
The plus-one side of a desert tile. A camel landing on an oasis moves one additional space forward and goes on top of any camels at the new destination. The tile owner earns one coin.

The minus-one side of a desert tile. A camel landing on a mirage moves one space backward and goes to the bottom of any stack on that space. The tile owner earns one coin. Can dramatically change race positions.
Each colored die has six faces: three ones, two twos, and one three. The average roll is approximately 1.83 spaces. Knowing the distribution helps estimate camel movement ranges.
The race ends immediately when any camel crosses or reaches space sixteen. The current leg is scored, then overall winner and loser bets are revealed and scored. The player with the most coins wins.
